items.title: Getafe stun Real Madrid on Satriano wonder-strike
items.summary: Real Madrid lost more ground at the top of LaLiga as a stunning first-half strike from Martín Satriano earned Getafe a shock 1-0 win at the Bernabeu.

items.title: Mourinho: 'Over' for Prestianni if guilty of abuse
items.summary: Jose Mourinho said Gianluca Prestianni will have no future at Benfica if he is found guilty of racially abusing Real Madrid's Vinícius Júnior.

items.title: USMNT's McKennie signs new Juve deal until 2030
items.summary: United States midfielder Weston McKennie has signed a new deal at Juventus until 2030, the Serie A club announced.

items.title: Fabregas wants 'little Bombonera' for Inter visit
items.summary: Cesc Fabregas has urged Como fans to turn their Giuseppe Sinigaglia Stadium into a "little Bombonera" for Tuesday's Coppa Italia tie against Inter Milan.

items.title: Middle East conflict puts Finalissima in doubt
items.summary: UEFA are closely monitoring the situation in the Middle East with respect to the Finalissima between Argentina and Spain scheduled for March 27 in Doha, Qatar.

items.title: Getafe raid Real Madrid at home in shock La Liga victory
items.summary: Martin Satriano’s superb first-half volley earned Getafe a shock 1-0 victory at Real Madrid in La Liga on Monday, a result that left Barcelona four points clear at the top. Real dominated possession from kick-off and controlled the game but failed to turn pressure into goals, squandering several chances in an ill-tempered contest. Continue reading...

items.title: Targett double sets Middlesbrough on the path to victory against Birmingham
items.summary: Matt Targett’s first-half double helped Middlesbrough return to winning ways with a 3-1 victory over Birmingham and further boost their hopes of promotion to the Premier League. Third-place Millwall’s triumph against Preston at the weekend cut the gap to just the one point coming into the match, but Kim Hellberg’s side responded to restore their four-point advantage in second place. Birmingham lost just their second league game at St Andrew’s since May 2024, meaning their chances of finishing in the playoffs have slipped further away. They remain eight points off the top six. Continue reading...

items.title: Benfica werkt met zege op zak naar alles-of-niets-clash met FC Porto toe
items.summary: Benfica trekt zijn been niet terug in de Portugese titelstrijd. De ploeg van José Mourinho richtte zich na de Champions League-eliminatie door Real Madrid weer op en won maandagavond de lastige uitwedstrijd bij Gil Vicente, mede door een goal van Vangelis Pavlidis. De Griek staat nu op 21 doelpunten in de Liga Portugal.

items.title: Captain Semenyo and go big in defence - FPL team of week
items.summary: With Erling Haaland possibly out this week, Antoine Semenyo becomes an even better FPL option given his recent goalscoring run for Manchester City.

items.title: Wereldgoal bezorgt Getafe sensationele zege in Bernabéu
items.summary: Getafe heeft maandagavond voor een sensatie gezorgd door te winnen op het veld van Real Madrid. Een buitengewoon fraaie goal van Martin Satriano maakte vlak voor rust het verschil in de streekderby. Real loopt na de tweede nederlaag op rij zeer dure averij op in de titelrace.

items.title: ‘Our first No 7’: Lynda Hale, pioneer who scored in England’s first women’s match, dies
items.summary: Southampton winger won Women’s FA Cup seven times Lionesses will wear black armbands against Ukraine Tributes are being paid after the death of the trailblazing footballer Lynda Hale, who scored in England’s first official women’s international fixture. Hale, who was also a seven-time winner of the Women’s FA Cup as part of the Southampton Women’s FC side that dominated the English game throughout the 1970s, started the Lionesses’ first formal game, against Scotland in November 1972. Continue reading...

items.title: Aanzienlijk hogere pot: dit is dit jaar het prijzengeld in de KNVB-beker
items.summary: De KNVB heeft de prijzenpot in de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker dit seizoen aanzienlijk opgeschroefd. De winnaar gaat naar huis met een half miljoen euro, een stijging van ruim 42 procent. Dit staat er op het spel voor NEC, PSV, AZ en Telstar.

items.title: Infantino’s idolisation of Trump has left football with blood on its hands | Barney Ronay
items.summary: The Fifa president’s sycophancy towards the US president has left the organisation facing a new nadir, but any reckoning seems a distant prospect Mr President. Fellow exco members. We’re going to need a bigger Board of Peace. How many mini‑pitches are we up to now? Gaza got 50 of them last month. What will it take to football-fix the global conflict being set in train by Fifa’s own Peace Prize Boy? A hundred mini-pitches? Four billion mini-pitches? All the mini‑pitches in the universe? In a more sane version of what we must, out of habit, call the real world, it would seem absurd to talk about sports administration in the context of the US, Iran and the airborne conflict being played out across the borders of their allies. Continue reading...

items.title: Postema straft gestuntel af en verbetert zijn topprestatie bij FC Groningen
items.summary: FC Emmen heeft maandagavond met een minimale marge van Jong FC Utrecht gewonnen. Romano Postema vierde zijn terugkeer met het enige doelpunt van de wedstrijd en heeft nu al vaker gescoord dan in zijn droomjaar bij FC Groningen.

items.title: Van Bommel lyrisch over Ihattaren: 'Hij was de beste speler van de Eredivisie'
items.summary: Mark van Bommel en Mohamed Ihattaren hebben nog geregeld contact. De Limburger is blij dat zijn voormalige protegé bij PSV zijn carrière, inmiddels bij Fortuna Sittard, weer de goede kant op krijgt.

items.title: Referees get a hard time but they have got the big calls right in the past couple of weeks | Chris Foy
items.summary: It was right to disallow Tottenham’s goal in the north London derby and it was also correct to rule out the goal that would have given Burnley a 4-4 draw against Brentford At the start of the season, clubs, fans and the media were told referees would be taking a proactive approach to holding in the penalty area, or grappling as some call it. We have since seen some really good examples of on-field referees making decisions where they have clearly identified holding offences and others where the VAR has recommended a review. But in the past six or eight weeks the issue seems to have intensified, with a change in tack in the Premier League, where clubs are loading the penalty area at every set piece. During the game between Arsenal and Chelsea on Sunday there was one corner where Declan Rice was holding Jorrel Hato, David Raya was being held, Gabriel Magalhães’s shirt was being pulled and Viktor Gyökeres was having a tug at Enzo Fernández. It was very messy. The players have a real responsibility with this because a lot of histrionics go with it. Players are going to ground easily, obscured by a crowd of people in the penalty area, and offences can be difficult to detect. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Clubs refused to look at me after my crash' - Antonio on Qatar move
items.summary: Michail Antonio says he had to "swallow his ego" after a near‑fatal car crash as he joins Qatari side Al‑Sailiya.

items.title: Sutton's predictions v singer-songwriter & Chelsea fan Noah Kahan
items.summary: BBC Sport football expert Chris Sutton takes on singer-songwriter Noah Kahan plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this week's midweek Premier League fixtures on Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday.

items.title: Will Iran play at World Cup co-hosted by USA? How Middle East crisis affects sport
items.summary: With the conflict between Iran and US-Israeli forces intensifying, Sky Sports looks at how the Middle East crisis is affecting sport...

items.title: Greaves drawn against Pilgrim at UK Open
items.summary: Beau Greaves has been drawn against Darryl Pilgrim in the opening round of the UK Open, where Luke Littler returns as defending champion.
